title: Sweet Fluffy Vietnamese Egg Omelette
description: A sweet and cozy Vietnamese dish featuring free-range eggs, perfect for a plant-based friendly meal. This omelette is made with sautéed onions and mushrooms, and is flavored with soy sauce and sesame oil.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 4-5 minutes.
In a large bowl, beat the eggs and season with salt, black pepper, and soy sauce.
Pour the egg mixture over the onion and mushroom mixture in the skillet.
Cook the eggs until the edges start to set, about 2-3 minutes.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the omelette towards the center.
Continue cooking the eggs until they are almost set, about 2-3 minutes more.
Use the spatula to fold the omelette in half and cook for an additional 30 seconds.
Slide the omelette out of the skillet onto a plate and garnish with chopped scallions and diced tofu if desired.
